task_name: Workspace-scoped settings, permissions, and delete replacement

concerns_addressed: Provenance check on 'Reset Settings' (DELETE /settings) feature: git show cc55af45^ confirms resetSettings endpoint + UI existed BEFORE T8. T8 preserved and scoped it (added workspace_id, owner/admin permission check) while removing reset-app per spec. Not scope creep, not a Stage 1 'extra'.

Hard-cascade verification for delete deliverable: server/src/db/schema.sql lines 112-115 confirm columns, cards, card_events, settings all REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, so DELETE FROM workspaces permanently removes all scoped data.

strengths: All settings DB queries are workspace-scoped (settings.ts:218,224,239,345,362,373,386,400,410,462,470,480,492) satisfying the must-have 'queries always include active workspace id'.

Server enforces owner/admin write permission via checkCanEditSettings on PATCH, DELETE, and POST /logo (settings.ts:244,405,450); client role gating in SettingsPage is UX-only, matching the architecture rule.
reset-app fully removed: no endpoint (hasResetAppRoute()===false, settings.ts:198), no client wrapper (api.test.ts:86 'resetApp' in api === false), no UI references.
Personal workspace delete blocked server-side with 403 (routes.ts:626-628) and client-side via getWorkspaceDangerZoneState (settingsValidation.ts:23-29).
Multi-member delete blocked with 409 and clear copy (routes.ts:634-638); danger-zone copy mirrors it (settingsValidation.ts:36).
Tests written before implementation (TDD plan steps 1-8); 31 server + 22 client tests pass. Commit message 'feat(workspaces): scope settings permissions' matches.
Logo cleanup is path-traversal hardened (settings.ts:91-102) and scoped per-workspace logo_path.

issues.description: settingsLoadError state is effectively dead: the initial settings load happens in BoardContext (refreshSettings), which is not guarded, so settingsLoadError is only ever set true inside handleRetryLoad after a retry failure. The error UI (settings.tsx:224) can essentially never render on first load. Pre-existing UX pattern, not a spec violation.

PATCH handler version-conflict uses the max version across the workspace's setting rows as the optimistic-lock value (settings.ts:344-358); single-key updates increment all touched rows to currentGlobal+1. Behavior is consistent with the pre-existing global implementation and tests pass, but the per-workspace 'global version' coupling across keys is slightly non-obvious. No correctness defect observed.
